name: Story — Definition of Ready (Base)
description: 7-criterion universal checklist confirming a story is independently deliverable in a single sprint. Domain extensions add criteria specific to a line of business (Data & Reporting, Capital Markets, etc.).

criteria.name: Template Fully Populated
criteria.description: All template fields populated — no empty placeholders or 'TBD' fields.
criteria.why: Empty fields surface mid-sprint as open questions and block the squad.
criteria.fix: Fill in the missing template fields before bringing the story to refinement.
criteria.good_example: Title, persona, capability, business outcome, source systems, business rules, ACs, and traceability all written out — no 'TBD' or blanks.

criteria.name: Acceptance Criteria are Binary and Testable
criteria.description: Acceptance criteria are stated as binary (pass/fail), independently testable conditions — not subjective ('looks right').
criteria.why: Subjective ACs cause demo rejection because PO and tester have different mental models.
criteria.fix: Rewrite each AC as a binary, testable statement (AC1, AC2... format) a tester can verify without ambiguity.
criteria.good_example: AC1: Output value matches reference within agreed tolerance. AC2: Filtering by [parameter] returns only matching records. AC3: Operation completes within agreed SLA.

criteria.name: Source Systems Confirmed Accessible
criteria.description: Upstream source systems, tables, feeds, or APIs the story touches are named and the squad has confirmed access.
criteria.why: Discovering mid-sprint that a developer can't reach the source forces a wait or scope cut.
criteria.fix: Name the source system, owner, refresh/cadence, and confirm the squad has working access.
criteria.good_example: Source: [system / table / API endpoint] · Owner: [team] · Refresh: [cadence]. Access confirmed by [squad lead] on [date].

criteria.name: Business Rules Unambiguous and Signed Off
criteria.description: Transformation logic, domain definitions, calculations, and edge cases are written out explicitly and signed off by the analyst.
criteria.why: Vague references to 'standard logic' cause rework when squads interpret them differently.
criteria.fix: Write out the exact calculation, key term definitions used by the team, and how edge cases (nulls, exclusions, rounding) are handled.
criteria.good_example: Defined: [term1] = ..., [term2] = ... Edge cases: nulls treated as ..., excluded records: ... Signed off by [analyst] on [date].

criteria.name: Linked to Parent Feature
criteria.description: The story is explicitly linked to a parent feature so the strategic intent is traceable in two clicks.
criteria.why: Stories without a parent feature can't be impact-analyzed when the feature scope changes.
criteria.fix: Link the story to its parent feature in the backlog tool. If no feature exists yet, define one before sprinting the story.
criteria.good_example: Parent feature: FEAT-1042 'Standardized Sub-Servicer Daily Activity File Ingestion'.

criteria.name: Three-Amigos Review Complete
criteria.description: PO, one developer, and one QA have walked through the story together and agreed it's ready.
criteria.why: Three-Amigos surfaces hidden assumptions before sprinting — cheaper than discovering them mid-sprint.
criteria.fix: Schedule a 15-min Three-Amigos walkthrough; capture sign-off from PO + Dev + QA before marking the story ready.
criteria.good_example: Three-Amigos completed [date] with PO ([name]), Dev ([name]), QA ([name]). No unresolved questions.

criteria.name: Sized — Pointed by the Squad
criteria.description: Story has been estimated in story points by the team during refinement or planning.
criteria.why: Unpointed stories cannot be planned into a sprint — they are invisible to capacity planning.
criteria.fix: Estimate the story in story points at the next refinement session.
